forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,45 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KSMXH3N2YKCNB3HGDYBF5E6B
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8280'
+title: 'Doctor: restrict install-stack to the 4 main doctored agents'
+---
+## What
+
+`mirdan::status::check_all` iterates every agent returned by `agents::get_detected_agents`, which means `sah doctor` and `mirdan doctor` show install-stack rows for every detected coding agent on disk (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Roo Code, GitHub Copilot, Continue, Amp, Zed AI, etc. — anything in `agents_default.yaml` whose detect rule fires).
+
+Per the user, only Claude Code, Zed AI, GitHub Copilot, and Codex are first-class doctored agents. Restrict the install-stack to that allowlist so detected-but-not-doctored agents do not produce doctor rows. The full registry stays available to `mirdan` itself (install, status, list); only the doctor's install-stack consumer changes.
+
+Approach: add a public constant or function in `mirdan::status` — e.g. `pub const DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS: &[&str] = &["claude-code", "zed-ai", "copilot", "codex"];` — and a sibling `check_all_doctored(config, scopes)` that filters detected agents by id before delegating. Update both consumers:
+
+- `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs::MirdanDoctor::check_install_stack` — use the doctored variant.
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::check_install_stack_with` — use the doctored variant.
+
+Do NOT delete `check_all`; it stays as the broad detector used by `mirdan status` (which shows everything by design — that's the "where are the packages" view).
+
+Files:
+- `crates/mirdan/src/status.rs` — add `DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS` and `check_all_doctored`.
+- `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs` — switch `check_install_stack` to `check_all_doctored`.
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs` — switch `check_install_stack_with` to `check_all_doctored`.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `mirdan::status::DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS` is exactly `["claude-code", "zed-ai", "copilot", "codex"]`.
+- [ ] `check_all_doctored(config, scopes)` returns rows only for those four ids.
+- [ ] `sah doctor` and `mirdan doctor` install-stack output never contains a row whose check name starts with an agent name outside the allowlist, even when that agent's detect rule fires.
+- [ ] `mirdan status` (the table command) still shows every detected agent (no behavior change there).
+- [ ] Existing install-stack tests in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs::tests` and `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::tests` still pass (update assertions if they referenced ids outside the allowlist).
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Add `test_check_all_doctored_filters_to_allowlist` in `crates/mirdan/src/status.rs::tests`: build an `AgentsConfig` containing one allowlisted agent plus one non-allowlisted agent (e.g. `cursor`), call `check_all_doctored(&config, &[InitScope::Project, InitScope::User])`, assert every returned `ComponentStatus.agent_id` is in `DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS`.
+- [ ] Add `test_check_install_stack_filters_to_allowlist` in `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::tests` driving `check_install_stack_with` with a synthetic config containing `cursor` and asserting no check name contains "Cursor".
+- [ ] Same shape in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs::tests`.
+- [ ] Test commands: `cargo test -p mirdan status::tests`, `cargo test -p mirdan doctor::tests`, `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli doctor::checks::tests`.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the three filtering tests first; they should fail because `check_all` returns the non-allowlisted agent. Then add the constant + `check_all_doctored` and switch consumers.
+
+## Depends on
+- 01KSMXH3N2YKCNB3HGDYBF5E6B (the augmented YAML for the 4 agents)
\ No newline at end of file

The full registry stays available to `mirdan` itself (install, status, list); only the doctor's install-stack consumer changes.

-Approach: add a public constant or function in `mirdan::status` — e.g. `pub const DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS: &[&str] = &["claude-code", "zed-ai", "copilot", "codex"];` — and a sibling `check_all_doctored(config, scopes)` that filters detected agents by id before delegating. Update both consumers:
-
-- `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs::MirdanDoctor::check_install_stack` — use the doctored variant.
-- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::check_install_stack_with` — use the doctored variant.

-## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `mirdan::status::DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS` is exactly `["claude-code", "zed-ai", "copilot", "codex"]`.
-- [ ] `check_all_doctored(config, scopes)` returns rows only for those four ids.
-- [ ] `sah doctor` and `mirdan doctor` install-stack output never contains a row whose check name starts with an agent name outside the allowlist, even when that agent's detect rule fires.

-- [ ] Existing install-stack tests in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs::tests` and `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::tests` still pass (update assertions if they referenced ids outside the allowlist).

-## Tests
-- [ ] Add `test_check_all_doctored_filters_to_allowlist` in `crates/mirdan/src/status.rs::tests`: build an `AgentsConfig` containing one allowlisted agent plus one non-allowlisted agent (e.g. `cursor`), call `check_all_doctored(&config, &[InitScope::Project, InitScope::User])`, assert every returned `ComponentStatus.agent_id` is in `DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS`.
-- [ ] Add `test_check_install_stack_filters_to_allowlist` in `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::tests` driving `check_install_stack_with` with a synthetic config containing `cursor` and asserting no check name contains "Cursor".
-- [ ] Same shape in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs::tests`.
-- [ ] Test commands: `cargo test -p mirdan status::tests`, `cargo test -p mirdan doctor::tests`, `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli doctor::checks::tests`.
-
-## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the three filtering tests first; they should fail because `check_all` returns the non-allowlisted agent. Then add the constant + `check_all_doctored` and switch consumers.

-## Depends on

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -5,41 +5,59 @@
 - 01KSMXH3N2YKCNB3HGDYBF5E6B
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '8280'
-title: 'Doctor: restrict install-stack to the 4 main doctored agents'
+title: 'Doctor: restrict install-stack to YAML-declared doctored agents'
 ---
 ## What

-`mirdan::status::check_all` iterates every agent returned by `agents::get_detected_agents`, which means `sah doctor` and `mirdan doctor` show install-stack rows for every detected coding agent on disk (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Roo Code, GitHub Copilot, Continue, Amp, Zed AI, etc. — anything in `agents_default.yaml` whose detect rule fires).
+`mirdan::status::check_all` iterates every agent returned by `agents::get_detected_agents`, which means `sah doctor` and `mirdan doctor` show install-stack rows for every detected coding agent on disk (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Cline, Roo Code, GitHub Copilot, Continue, Amp, Zed AI, etc.). Per the user, only Claude Code, Zed AI, GitHub Copilot, and Codex are first-class doctored agents. Restrict the install-stack to the agents that opt in via YAML.

-Per the user, only Claude Code, Zed AI, GitHub Copilot, and Codex are first-class doctored agents. Restrict the install-stack to that allowlist so detected-but-not-doctored agents do not produce doctor rows. The full registry stays available to `mirdan` itself (install, status, list); only the doctor's install-stack consumer changes.
+Drive the allowlist from `agents_default.yaml`, not a hardcoded Rust array. Add an optional `doctor: bool` field (default `false`) to the `AgentDef` schema:
+
+```yaml
+- id: claude-code
+  name: Claude Code
+  doctor: true
+  ...
+```

-Approach: add a public constant or function in `mirdan::status` — e.g. `pub const DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS: &[&str] = &["claude-code", "zed-ai", "copilot", "codex"];` — and a sibling `check_all_doctored(config, scopes)` that filters detected agents by id before delegating. Update both consumers:
+Only agents with `doctor: true` participate in the install-stack. This keeps the registry and the doctor coupled by data, not code — adding a fifth doctored agent in the future is a YAML edit, not a Rust change.

-- `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs::MirdanDoctor::check_install_stack` — use the doctored variant.
-- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::check_install_stack_with` — use the doctored variant.
+Approach:

-Do NOT delete `check_all`; it stays as the broad detector used by `mirdan status` (which shows everything by design — that's the "where are the packages" view).
+1. Extend `crates/mirdan/src/agents.rs::AgentDef` with `#[serde(default)] pub doctor: bool` (a missing field deserializes to `false`).
+2. In `crates/mirdan/src/agents_default.yaml`, set `doctor: true` on `claude-code`, `zed-ai`, `copilot`, `codex`. Every other entry stays as-is (the missing field defaults to `false`). The path-expansion edits from card **01KSMXH3N2YKCNB3HGDYBF5E6B** are a precondition for the YAML changes here landing cleanly — coordinate with that card so both edit the same YAML entries once.
+3. Add `mirdan::status::check_all_doctored(config, scopes)` that filters by `AgentDef.doctor` before delegating. No `DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS` constant — the YAML is the source of truth.
+4. Update both consumers to use the new entry point:
+   - `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs::MirdanDoctor::check_install_stack`
+   - `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::check_install_stack_with`
+5. `mirdan status` (the table command) stays on the broad `check_all` — `mirdan` is "where are the packages" and shows everything.

 Files:
-- `crates/mirdan/src/status.rs` — add `DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS` and `check_all_doctored`.
-- `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs` — switch `check_install_stack` to `check_all_doctored`.
-- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs` — switch `check_install_stack_with` to `check_all_doctored`.
+- `crates/mirdan/src/agents.rs` — add `doctor: bool` field with `#[serde(default)]`.
+- `crates/mirdan/src/agents_default.yaml` — set `doctor: true` on the 4 agents.
+- `crates/mirdan/src/status.rs` — add `check_all_doctored`.
+- `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs` — switch to `check_all_doctored`.
+- `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs` — switch to `check_all_doctored`.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `mirdan::status::DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS` is exactly `["claude-code", "zed-ai", "copilot", "codex"]`.
-- [ ] `check_all_doctored(config, scopes)` returns rows only for those four ids.
-- [ ] `sah doctor` and `mirdan doctor` install-stack output never contains a row whose check name starts with an agent name outside the allowlist, even when that agent's detect rule fires.
+- [ ] `AgentDef` has a public field `pub doctor: bool` with `#[serde(default)]` so older configs without the field still parse (defaulting to `false`).
+- [ ] `agents_default.yaml` sets `doctor: true` on exactly `claude-code`, `zed-ai`, `copilot`, and `codex`; every other entry omits the field (or sets `false`).
+- [ ] `check_all_doctored(config, scopes)` returns only rows whose `AgentDef.doctor` is `true`.
+- [ ] `sah doctor` and `mirdan doctor` install-stack output contains rows only for those four agents, regardless of what else is detected on disk.
 - [ ] `mirdan status` (the table command) still shows every detected agent (no behavior change there).
-- [ ] Existing install-stack tests in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs::tests` and `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::tests` still pass (update assertions if they referenced ids outside the allowlist).
+- [ ] No `DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS` constant or other hardcoded id list anywhere in Rust — the only allowlist is the YAML field.
+- [ ] Existing install-stack tests in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs::tests` and `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::tests` still pass (update assertions if they referenced ids outside the YAML allowlist).

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Add `test_check_all_doctored_filters_to_allowlist` in `crates/mirdan/src/status.rs::tests`: build an `AgentsConfig` containing one allowlisted agent plus one non-allowlisted agent (e.g. `cursor`), call `check_all_doctored(&config, &[InitScope::Project, InitScope::User])`, assert every returned `ComponentStatus.agent_id` is in `DOCTORED_AGENT_IDS`.
-- [ ] Add `test_check_install_stack_filters_to_allowlist` in `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::tests` driving `check_install_stack_with` with a synthetic config containing `cursor` and asserting no check name contains "Cursor".
+- [ ] Add `test_agent_def_doctor_field_defaults_to_false` in `crates/mirdan/src/agents.rs::tests`: parse a YAML entry that omits `doctor`, assert `doctor == false`. Parse one that sets `doctor: true`, assert `doctor == true`.
+- [ ] Add `test_agents_default_yaml_doctors_exactly_four_agents` in `crates/mirdan/src/agents.rs::tests`: load `agents_default.yaml`, collect `AgentDef`s where `doctor == true`, assert the set is exactly `{claude-code, zed-ai, copilot, codex}`.
+- [ ] Add `test_check_all_doctored_filters_by_doctor_field` in `crates/mirdan/src/status.rs::tests`: build a synthetic `AgentsConfig` with two agents (one `doctor: true`, one `doctor: false`, both detectable), call `check_all_doctored(&config, &[InitScope::Project, InitScope::User])`, assert every returned `ComponentStatus.agent_id` belongs to a `doctor: true` agent.
+- [ ] Add `test_check_install_stack_only_emits_doctored_agents` in `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs::tests`: drive `check_install_stack_with` with a synthetic config containing `cursor` (no `doctor` field) and assert no check name contains "Cursor".
 - [ ] Same shape in `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs::tests`.
-- [ ] Test commands: `cargo test -p mirdan status::tests`, `cargo test -p mirdan doctor::tests`, `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli doctor::checks::tests`.
+- [ ] Test commands: `cargo test -p mirdan agents::tests`, `cargo test -p mirdan status::tests`, `cargo test -p mirdan doctor::tests`, `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli doctor::checks::tests`.

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` — write the three filtering tests first; they should fail because `check_all` returns the non-allowlisted agent. Then add the constant + `check_all_doctored` and switch consumers.
+- Use `/tdd` — write the YAML-driven filtering tests first; they should fail because `AgentDef.doctor` does not exist yet. Then add the field, set it on the four agents in YAML, add `check_all_doctored`, and switch consumers.

 ## Depends on
-- 01KSMXH3N2YKCNB3HGDYBF5E6B (the augmented YAML for the 4 agents) #init-doctor
\ No newline at end of file
+- 01KSMXH3N2YKCNB3HGDYBF5E6B (agents_default.yaml edits for the 4 agents)
\ No newline at end of file

 ## Depends on
-- 01KSMXH3N2YKCNB3HGDYBF5E6B (agents_default.yaml edits for the 4 agents)
\ No newline at end of file
+- 01KSMXH3N2YKCNB3HGDYBF5E6B (agents_default.yaml edits for the 4 agents)
+
+## Review Findings (2026-05-27 11:33)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/mirdan/src/doctor.rs:280-301` and `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/doctor/checks.rs:78-100` — In `test_check_install_stack_only_emits_doctored_agents`, both `claude-code` (doctored) and `cursor` (undoctored) use `/nonexistent/...` detect paths. Because neither detects, `get_detected_agents` triggers the `claude-code` fallback in `agents.rs:227-235` and `cursor` never enters the input set to `check_all_doctored` — the doctor filter is never actually exercised. The "no check name contains 'Cursor'" assertion would pass even against the unfiltered `check_all`. Fix by creating real temp dirs (as `status.rs::test_check_all_doctored_filters_by_doctor_field` already does) so both agents detect and the filter is what excludes cursor. The `status.rs` test does cover the filter correctly, so the regression coverage is not zero, but these two consumer tests don't add the boundary they claim.
+
+### Nits
+- [ ] `crates/mirdan/src/status.rs:260-270` — `check_all_doctored` duplicates the scope/component iteration loop with `check_all` (lines 236-245). Three lines of shared body is below the threshold where extraction pays off, but if a third caller appears, fold the loop into a private `check_for_agents(&[DetectedAgent], &[InitScope])` helper that both wrappers delegate to.
\ No newline at end of file
